subtitle: Knee pain — location not yet pinpointed

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Knee pain is the most common running injury — often driven by hip weakness

mild.can_run_note: Yes — reduce intensity and identify the specific location
why: General knee joint stress from accumulated mileage
Multiple structures fatiguing from training load
Hip weakness transmitting extra load to the knee
relief: Ice knee after every run for 15 min
Identify whether pain is front, outer, or inner knee and use specific panel
prevention: Hip and glute strengthening — the most effective knee injury prevention

moderate.description: Knee pain affecting running form or pace
moderate.can_run_note: Easy flat runs only — no hills or speed work
why: Specific structure being irritated — needs identification
relief: Try to localise pain: kneecap, outer, inner, or below kneecap
prevention: Use specific pain panels to target the correct structure

severe.description: Significant knee pain, swelling, or instability
why: Undiagnosed knee pathology requiring proper assessment
Possible structural damage to meniscus, ligament, or cartilage
relief: Rest and see a physiotherapist for diagnosis
Imaging if swelling or instability present
prevention: Return to running only with a clear diagnosis and structured plan

see_physio_if: Swelling inside or around the knee
Knee giving way or feeling unstable
Pain at night or at rest
No improvement after 1 week of reduced load
